police_fir.name: First Information Report (FIR)
police_fir.purpose: Official document that starts the criminal justice process for a cognisable offence
police_fir.where_to_get: Any police station — they must provide it free of charge after registering your complaint
police_fir.notes: You are legally entitled to a free copy of your FIR under Section 154 CrPC. If police refuse, escalate to the SP.

police_fir.name: Written Complaint to Superintendent of Police
police_fir.purpose: Escalation when local police refuse to register your FIR
police_fir.where_to_get: No official form — write your own complaint letter and send by registered post to the SP office
police_fir.notes: Under Section 154(3) CrPC. Keep the postal receipt as proof of submission.

police_fir.name: Section 156(3) Complaint to Magistrate
police_fir.purpose: Direct complaint to a magistrate ordering police to register an FIR
police_fir.where_to_get: File at the filing counter of your nearest Judicial Magistrate Court
police_fir.notes: No lawyer required. Write your complaint, attach any evidence, submit at court counter.

police_fir.name: Bail Application
police_fir.purpose: Application for release on bail for a bailable or non-bailable offence
police_fir.where_to_get: Available at the court filing counter or through your lawyer
police_fir.notes: For bailable offences, bail is a right — police cannot refuse.

eviction_housing.name: Rent Control Court Complaint
eviction_housing.purpose: Complaint against illegal eviction or landlord harassment
eviction_housing.where_to_get: Rent Control Court in your city (called Rent Authority in some states)
eviction_housing.notes: Procedure varies by state. Maharashtra: approach the Rent Court under Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999. Karnataka: approach the Rent Control Court under Karnataka Rent Act 2001.

eviction_housing.name: RERA Complaint Form
eviction_housing.purpose: Complaint against a builder or developer for delayed possession, false promises, or defects
eviction_housing.where_to_get: State RERA portal — each state has its own
eviction_housing.notes: Maharashtra: MahaRERA. Karnataka: K-RERA at rera.karnataka.gov.in. Tamil Nadu: TNRERA at tnrera.in

eviction_housing.name: Police Complaint for Illegal Eviction
eviction_housing.purpose: FIR against landlord for forcible/illegal eviction
eviction_housing.notes: Illegal eviction (removing tenant without court order) is a criminal offence. File an FIR under IPC Section 441 (criminal trespass).

workplace_salary.name: Labour Court Complaint (Form A)
workplace_salary.purpose: Complaint for unpaid wages, wrongful termination, or labour law violations
workplace_salary.where_to_get: Office of the Labour Commissioner in your district
workplace_salary.notes: Under the Industrial Disputes Act. File within 3 years of the violation.

workplace_salary.name: Payment of Wages Authority Complaint
workplace_salary.purpose: Complaint specifically for unpaid or delayed wages
workplace_salary.where_to_get: Office of the Payment of Wages Authority / Labour Commissioner
workplace_salary.notes: Under Payment of Wages Act 1936. Faster than Labour Court for straightforward unpaid salary cases.

workplace_salary.name: PF Grievance Form
workplace_salary.purpose: Complaint for Provident Fund not deposited by employer
workplace_salary.notes: You can also call EPFO helpline at 1800-118-005 (toll free).

workplace_salary.name: Sexual Harassment Complaint (ICC)
workplace_salary.purpose: Formal complaint to Internal Complaints Committee under POSH Act
workplace_salary.where_to_get: Your company's HR department or Internal Complaints Committee
workplace_salary.notes: Every company with 10+ employees must have an ICC. If none exists, file with Local Complaints Committee at District Collector office.

consumer_fraud.name: Consumer Complaint Form (Form A — CPA 2019)
consumer_fraud.purpose: Formal complaint against seller, service provider, or e-commerce platform
consumer_fraud.where_to_get: District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission in your district, or file online
consumer_fraud.notes: Claims up to Rs 50 lakh go to District Commission. Rs 50 lakh to 2 crore go to State Commission. Above 2 crore go to National Commission. Filing fee is minimal.

consumer_fraud.name: National Consumer Helpline Complaint
consumer_fraud.purpose: Quick complaint registration before formal legal proceedings
consumer_fraud.notes: NCH mediates between consumer and company. Faster than court. Use this first before filing in District Commission.

consumer_fraud.name: Cyber Crime Complaint
consumer_fraud.purpose: Complaint for online fraud, phishing, or digital financial fraud
consumer_fraud.notes: File within 24-48 hours of the fraud for best chance of recovery. Also call helpline 1930 for financial cyber fraud.

consumer_fraud.name: RBI Banking Ombudsman Complaint
consumer_fraud.purpose: Complaint against bank for fraud, unauthorized transactions, or poor service
consumer_fraud.notes: Free to file. Must first complain to bank and wait 30 days before approaching ombudsman.

domestic_violence.name: Domestic Incident Report (DIR)
domestic_violence.purpose: Official record of domestic violence incident — the first document in the legal process
domestic_violence.where_to_get: Protection Officer at your district collectorate, or any registered NGO or shelter home
domestic_violence.notes: Under Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act 2005. Filing a DIR is free and does not automatically mean court proceedings.

domestic_violence.name: Application for Protection Order
domestic_violence.purpose: Court order preventing the abuser from contacting or approaching you
domestic_violence.where_to_get: File at the Magistrate Court with help from a Protection Officer or lawyer
domestic_violence.notes: The magistrate can grant an emergency protection order the same day. Contact Protection Officer first — they assist for free.

domestic_violence.name: Application for Residence Order
domestic_violence.purpose: Court order allowing you to stay in the shared household even if you don't own it
domestic_violence.where_to_get: Magistrate Court — file along with or after Protection Order application
domestic_violence.notes: Even if the house is in the husband's name, you have a right to reside there under the DV Act.

domestic_violence.name: One Stop Centre Registration
domestic_violence.purpose: Access free shelter, medical, legal, and police assistance in one place
domestic_violence.where_to_get: One Stop Centres (Sakhi Centres) are in every district — contact district collectorate
domestic_violence.notes: Call Women Helpline 181 or 1091 to locate your nearest One Stop Centre. Available 24/7.

land_property.name: Revenue Court Complaint
land_property.purpose: Complaint for land encroachment, boundary disputes, or illegal possession
land_property.where_to_get: Tahsildar or Sub-Divisional Magistrate office in your tehsil
land_property.notes: Faster and cheaper than civil court for land disputes. Bring your land records (7/12 extract or RTC).

land_property.name: 7/12 Extract (Satbara Utara)
land_property.purpose: Official record of land ownership and cultivation — essential for all land disputes
land_property.where_to_get: Talathi office or online via state land records portal
land_property.notes: Maharashtra: MahaBhulekh. Karnataka: Bhoomi portal at landrecords.karnataka.gov.in. Tamil Nadu: TNEGA at eservices.tn.gov.in

land_property.name: Civil Suit for Injunction
land_property.purpose: Court order stopping someone from building on or using your land
land_property.where_to_get: File through a lawyer at the Civil Court for your jurisdiction
land_property.notes: Requires a lawyer. File for a temporary injunction first — can be granted within days to stop ongoing construction.

court_bail.name: Regular Bail Application
court_bail.purpose: Application for bail after arrest in a non-bailable offence
court_bail.where_to_get: File through a lawyer at the Sessions Court or the court where your case is being heard
court_bail.notes: If Sessions Court refuses, you can apply to the High Court. Legal aid lawyers available free through NALSA at 15100.

court_bail.name: Anticipatory Bail Application (Section 438 CrPC)
court_bail.purpose: Bail granted BEFORE arrest — protects you if you fear arrest
court_bail.where_to_get: Sessions Court or High Court — file through a lawyer
court_bail.notes: Must be filed before arrest. Once granted, you cannot be arrested for that offence without the court's permission.

court_bail.name: Default Bail Application (Section 167(2) CrPC)
court_bail.purpose: Bail as a right if charge sheet not filed within 60 or 90 days
court_bail.where_to_get: File at the Magistrate Court yourself or through a lawyer
court_bail.notes: You MUST apply for this — it is not automatic. 60 days for serious offences (death/life imprisonment), 90 days for others.

court_bail.name: Legal Aid Application (NALSA)
court_bail.purpose: Free lawyer provided by the state for those who cannot afford one
court_bail.where_to_get: District Legal Services Authority (DLSA) at your district court, or call NALSA at 15100
court_bail.notes: Available to anyone arrested, any woman, any person with disability, or anyone with annual income below Rs 1 lakh.

government_schemes.name: PM-KISAN Grievance Form
government_schemes.purpose: Complaint for non-receipt of PM-KISAN farmer income support payments
government_schemes.where_to_get: PM-KISAN portal online or Common Service Centre (CSC) in your village
government_schemes.notes: Check your payment status first on the portal. Common issues: wrong Aadhaar linkage, wrong bank account. Can be fixed at nearest CSC.

government_schemes.name: MGNREGA Job Card Application
government_schemes.purpose: Registration for 100 days of guaranteed employment under MGNREGA
government_schemes.where_to_get: Gram Panchayat office in your village
government_schemes.notes: Every rural household is entitled to a job card. If denied, file written complaint with Block Development Officer.

government_schemes.name: Aadhaar Grievance Form
government_schemes.purpose: Complaint for Aadhaar-related issues — wrong details, biometric failure, benefit denial
government_schemes.where_to_get: UIDAI self-service portal or nearest Aadhaar Seva Kendra
government_schemes.notes: Call UIDAI helpline 1947 for immediate assistance. If benefit is denied due to Aadhaar failure, you are still entitled to the benefit by alternative ID.

government_schemes.name: Ration Card Grievance (PDS Complaint)
government_schemes.purpose: Complaint for denial of ration entitlements or ration card issues
government_schemes.where_to_get: District Food and Civil Supplies office, or state PDS portal
government_schemes.notes: Under the National Food Security Act, every eligible household is entitled to subsidised grain. Denial is a legal violation.
